summary: OpenCode goal state should preserve Codex parity fields tokenBudget/tokensUsed/timeUsedSeconds and the full status set, with budget_limited for configured cap exhaustion and usage_limited for external runtime/provider usage refusal.
risk: Copying OpenHuman UI statuses directly would erase blocked and usage_limited.

summary: Account usage only after completed turn/message events, only for active goals, and only when expectedGoalID matches the persisted goalId; transition to budget_limited when tokensUsed reaches tokenBudget.
risk: message.updated may fire multiple times for one assistant turn, so implementation needs lastAccountedMessageID or per-message idempotence.

summary: maybeContinueGoal must refuse autonomous continuation unless the goal is active and still under tokenBudget; limited goals may inject a summary/blocker directive but must not call session.prompt.
risk: OpenCode mid-turn hard-stop parity is unverified; v1 should enforce pre-continuation and post-turn caps only.

summary: Changed objective mints a new goalId and resets counters; same objective preserves counters and only changes budget; raising budget above tokensUsed reactivates a budget_limited goal.
risk: Thin-router argument parsing can confuse numeric objective text with budget unless the command contract is strict.

summary: Installed OpenCode plugin types prove generic event hooks and system transforms, but do not document token fields for message.updated; mk-goal must use tolerant extraction and expose usageSource=unavailable when accounting cannot be trusted.
risk: Without real token payload smoke testing, counters may stay zero or undercount until a persisted OpenCode usage source is wired.
